title: Journaling
learning_outcomes: Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Write-ahead file commits logging, Crash consistency restoration algorithms.

content_sections.title: Journaling
content_sections.content:
### Kernel Architecture & Memory Virtualization
Operating systems govern resource virtualization, running system code in protected memory environments.

#### System Call Traps
Users run in user mode (**Ring 3**), and the kernel runs in kernel mode (**Ring 0**). A user program triggers a system call using a hardware instruction trap (e.g. `syscall` in x86-64). This halts execution, switches the privilege ring to Ring 0, saves registers, and jumps to the address mapped in the kernel's **System Call Vector Table**.

#### Context Switch Internals
A context switch swaps executing threads. The kernel:
1. Saves user registers to the thread's kernel stack.
2. Swaps the CPU's stack pointer to the target thread's stack.
3. Swaps the MMU page table pointer (registers like `CR3` in x86) to point to the new process's page directory, invalidating the Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) unless Address Space Identifiers (ASID) are used.

#### Peterson's Mutual Exclusion
For two threads $T_0, T_1$, mutual exclusion is guaranteed by:
```c
flag[i] = true;
turn = j;
while (flag[j] && turn == j);
// Critical Section
flag[i] = false;
```
Proof: If both enter while, `turn` can only equal one value ($0$ or $1$), forcing one thread to spin until the other exits the critical section.

### Asymptotic and Spatial Complexity Analysis
We summarize the computational characteristics for Journaling implementations in the following reference matrix:

| Process/Operation | Worst-Case Time | Average-Case Time | Space Complexity |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Core Logic Execution | $O(N)$ | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ |
| Memory Operations | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(N)$ |

callouts.title: Common Trap in Journaling
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Journaling is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.
